sprint: World-Owned Text Objects Finally Honour the Resize Grid
task: the user's own biggest-bug pick from a reported list, framed directly: "we will take the biggest bug. honoring the story author can adjust this grid. lets finalize it for any text object." A moveable World-owned Text Layer (a Museum Caption-style object) could already be moved on canvas, but selecting one never showed the 8-corner resize grid every other moveable Scene Object kind gets, and nothing downstream would have read the resulting size even if it had. Three-part fix: renderer/slideRenderer.js's _supportsResize() widened to include type:'text' (gated by the same locked/moveable check every other kind already uses); _layerDrawText() taught to read a size override and derive a uniform scale (applied to both font size and wrap width, then re-wrapped) rather than silently ignoring it; js/app.js's resize-drag aspect-lock extended to every handle (not just sticker corners) for a text-type element, since canvas text has no sensible non-uniform stretch in this renderer's wrap+font-size model. SceneEngine.setSize/.setPosition needed zero changes -- already fully generic. Verified via a new Playwright suite (a moveable and a locked synthetic Text Layer, a real mouse-driven drag on the 'se' handle writing a genuine size override and visibly growing the rendered text, durable across a redraw) plus full regression -- the existing bug332_334_335 verification and a real Museum Gallery .vtheme file's own render both confirmed byte-identical to a git-stash baseline for the no-override case, and every other test failure hit along the way was independently reconfirmed via git stash to already exist on the unmodified codebase (a pre-existing sticker/place drag bug, and older scripts predating the Traveller Gateway). Both goldenBuild.js suites pass unchanged. See CLAUDE.md's own entry for the full account.
